title: Leaning On Each Other
prose: O.W.L. year presses on everyone at once — the same tight shoulders in the library, the same too-many-books-for-one-bag, the same short tempers by ten o'clock at night — and somewhere in the middle of it, the friends who'll actually get you through it make themselves known.
Not through any single grand gesture. Just showing up. A shared table in the library, someone else's notes when yours don't make sense, a reason to laugh for five minutes before going back to it.

choices.label: You lean on them, and let them lean on you.
You mostly try to power through it alone.
